current_implemented_build: V56 Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
patch_type: Trust model registry and score explanation layer over V55.6 interaction reliability
release_chain_status: V56 is the current implemented build. It loads after V55.6 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It adds model explanations for visible scores while preserving the registry-rendered radar-constellation TrustMap, thick neon-blue CyberShield Core shield boundary, baseline Layer 1 operating domains, Purpose Protocol integration, and no-new-top-level-tabs rule. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V56. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
core_thesis: CyberShield evaluates whether the information behind a critical action or claim can be trusted before the organization acts, cites, shares, briefs, teaches, or relies on it.
v56_changes: shows inputs, weights, thresholds, evidence requirements, and limitations
keeps scores advisory unless validated otherwise

trust_model_registry.boundary: Demo-directional advisory models only. No certification, live evidence retrieval, legal determination, or production-calibrated scoring.

trustmap_interaction_layer.boundary: Static advisory navigation only. Does not create tickets, retrieve evidence, send notifications, or trigger live workflows.

internet_trust_engine_future_track.sequence_note: Do not implement before TrustMap registry, visual stabilization, interaction reliability, model transparency, and evidence/proof layers are stable.

recommended_next_build.label: V56.1 Evidence and Assumption Register
goals: separate known evidence, missing evidence, stale evidence, assumptions, and verification needs
link evidence states to TrustMap objects and score explanations

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live evidence retrieval, internet claim verification, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Model explanations are demo-directional and not statistically validated
Model buttons are static explanatory UI, not live calculations
Runtime, Evidence, and Proof Pack routing does not create tickets, send notifications, or retrieve live evidence
Internet Trust Engine is documented as a future requirement track but is not yet implemented
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
